entry.title: 360° of SAP Analytics Cloud Performance Optimization
entry.content:
The importance of performance in reporting and analytics cannot be explained away. Efficient performance optimization in reporting ensures timely insights and enhances decision-making processes.  

To adequately address this crucial issue, we want to introduce our brand-new free learning journey, Managing Performance in SAP Analytics Cloud, designed to equip you with valuable skills in optimizing performance within SAP Analytics Cloud. 

This learning journey covers a comprehensive range of topics to elevate your performance optimization skills.  

  1. You will start by understanding the SAP Analytics Cloud architecture and its impact on performance. 
  2. Next, you will dive into performance troubleshooting using real-world scenarios and uncovering individual metrics to analyze performance.  
  3. The learning journey also includes an exploration of key tools used in performance analysis and measurement. 
  4. You will explore the relationship between story design and performance and dig deeper, designing performance optimized data visualization using pages and widgets.  
  5. You will use specific settings and features that will optimize performance during data loading. 
  6. Finally, you will explore scripting performance by analyzing the story script to identify and solve performance issues.  

We invite you to join this advanced learning journey and master the art of performance optimization in SAP Analytics Cloud. 

If you want to complement your performance skill set with an expert-guided hands-on session, explore our German SAP Data & Analytics Forum Agenda in November 2024.    

SAP Product Learning CoE Analytics

entry.title: Use SAP CAP SAPUI5 Smart Filter to Show Value Help that are not Associated Entitites
entry.content:
How to Use SAP CAP and SAPUI5 Smart Filter to Show Value Help for Fields that are not Associated Entities

In cases where you need to allow the user to select values through a Value Help for a specific field that is not related to another table, you can achieve this behavior in the following way:

1. Create a View that Contains the Values for the Value Help

First, you need to create a view that only contains the values you want to display in the Value Help. This view should perform a SELECT on the entity from which you need to retrieve the values and use a GROUP BY to avoid repeating information.

 

@CDS.odata.valuelist
view Modules as
    select from GlobalSetting {
        key GlobalSettingModule
    }
    where
        GlobalSettingModule is not null
    group by
        GlobalSettingModule;

 

2. Add @Common Annotations in the Main View

In the view, entity, or projection that you are using to build the Smart Table, you need to add @Common annotations. These annotations will serve as input for CAP to determine that the previously created view should be used for the Value Help.

 

view ViewGlobalSettingPatterns as
    select from GlobalSetting {
        *,
        @Common.ValueList.CollectionPath: 'Module'
        @Common.ValueList.Parameters    : [{
            $Type            : 'Common.ValueListParameterInOut',
            LocalDataProperty: 'GlobalSettingModule',
            ValueListProperty: 'GlobalSettingModule'
        }, {
            $Type            : 'Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly',
            ValueListProperty: 'GlobalSettingModule'
        }]
        GlobalSettingModule
    }
    where
        GlobalSettingClassification = 'PATTERN';

 

3. Expose the Views in the Service

You need to expose the new views so that the service can access them and use them as a data source.

 

service MasterDataService {
    entity GlobalSetting             as projection on model.GlobalSetting;
    @readonly
    entity ViewGlobalSettingPatterns as projection on model.ViewGlobalSettingPatterns;
    @readonly
    @CDS.odata.valuelist
    entity Type                      as projection on model.Types;
    @readonly
    entity Module                    as projection on model.Modules;
}

 

4. Verify the OData Metadata

It is important to verify that the metadata correctly shows the annotations. The CollectionPath is the key point since it tells CAP which resource will be used to display the data in the Value Help.

The metadata should look something like this:

 

<Annotations xmlns="http://docs.oasis-open.org/odata/ns/edm" Target="MasterDataService.ViewGlobalSettingPatterns/GlobalSettingModule">
    <Annotation Term="Common.ValueList">
        <Record Type="Common.ValueListType">
            <PropertyValue Property="CollectionPath" String="Module"/>
            <PropertyValue Property="Parameters">
                <Collection>
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterInOut">
                        <PropertyValue Property="LocalDataProperty" PropertyPath="GlobalSettingModule"/>
                        <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="GlobalSettingModule"/>
                    </Record>
                    <Record Type="Common.ValueListParameterDisplayOnly">
                        <PropertyValue Property="ValueListProperty" String="GlobalSettingModule"/>
                    </Record>
                </Collection>
            </PropertyValue>
        </Record>
    </Annotation>
</Annotations>

 

5. Configure the Filter in the Smart Component

Finally, you just need to configure the Smart Filter, where you specify which field will display the Value Help.

 

<smartFilterBar:ControlConfiguration
    key="GlobalSettingModule"
    label="Module"
    visibleInAdvancedArea="true"
    hasValueHelpDialog="true"/>

 

 

Result

Anthony Ramos Romero.

entry.title: Re: [FSCM Collections]: Extend Manage Customer Line Items(App F0711) – OData MPC & DPC Code Enha
entry.content:
hi @robinthakral ,
had similar requirements, long ago.

Have some notes, where I obviously extended the CDS view and brute-force-inserted the fields in supporting DDIC-structures to get F0711 working. yes, NOT clean core, just dirty stuff to get customer happy.

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewAppendName: 'ZEIARLINEITEM'
@EndUserText.label: 'Custom fields for F0711'


/* Requirement:
   Additional columns in F0711.

   Problem:
   SAP Key User Extensibility (TCODE SCFD_REGISTRY)
   does not support code-free extension of F0711 yet.
   only in S/4HANA Cloud.

   Remedy:
   extend the view with manual magic. */


extend view I_ARLineItem with ZE_I_ARLineItem
  association [0..1] to bseg as _BSEG on  _BSEG.bukrs = P_ARLineItem_0.companycode
                                      and _BSEG.belnr = P_ARLineItem_0.accountingdocument
                                      and _BSEG.gjahr = P_ARLineItem_0.fiscalyear
                                      and _BSEG.docln = P_ARLineItem_0.accountingdocumentitem


  association [0..1] to bkpf as _BKPF on  _BKPF.bukrs = P_ARLineItem_0.companycode
                                      and _BKPF.belnr = P_ARLineItem_0.accountingdocument
                                      and _BKPF.gjahr = P_ARLineItem_0.fiscalyear

{
  _BSEG.zz1_salesteam_jei,

  _BKPF.bktxt    as zzBKTXT,
  _BKPF.xref1_hd as zzXREF1_HD,
  _BKPF.xref2_hd as zzXREF2_HD

}


my notes contain the name of a structure "FAR_IFIARLINEITEM_EX" as well. But I was lazy with full documentation...

in fact, F0711 is a complete clusterf*ck in terms of "add customer fields". And almost every customer needs this app WITH customer fields. SAP should do better and deliver a modernized variant of this app.

regards,
Christian
 
p.s. you wrote "Fiori App F12". seems to be a typo.

entry.title: Re: Advice on Reducing DHCDC_RTSTORE Table Size in S/4HANA
entry.content:
@Savio_Dmello
Thanks for editing the comment - appreciated.

The data of DHCDC_RTSTORE should always be deleted by the application - also in the case of failures - it is not necessary to manually delete data.
If data gets stuck its either a bug in the application or the replication was paused or disconnected from the source for some reason.
In this case there will be a cleanup performed after the defined retention time.
Hence, if the table is growing it is either a bug (-> support incident), or a misconfiguration of the retention.

entry.title: Neue UCC Angebote zum Thema Process Automation
entry.content:
Liebe Lehrenden in DACH,

es freut mich die nachfolgenden Information zu neuen Lernangeboten der UCCs mit Ihnen zu teilen.

Mit freundlichen Grüßen

Andre Biener

Director DACH, SAP University Alliances

******************

Neue SAP Process Automation Lernumgebung

Die neu entwickelte SAP Process Automation Lernumgebung verwendet den SAP-Cloud-Service SAP Build Process Automation. Er ist Teil der Low-code/No-code-Lösungsfamilie SAP Build. In Zukunft sollen weitere Komponenten wie SAP Build Apps und SAP Build Code zur Verfügung gestellt werden.

SAP Build Process Automation dient der Teil- oder Vollautomatisierung einfacher, meist repetitiver menschlicher Aufgaben. Intelligente Prozessautomatisierung soll dabei die Effizienz einfacher Betriebsprozesse verbessern, den Durchsatz und die Durchlaufgeschwindigkeit erhöhen sowie menschliche Fehler vermeiden. Dabei können in SAP Build Process Automation Prozesse oder Automationen modelliert, ausgeführt und ausgewertet werden.

Das entwickelte Curriculum verwendet das bekannte Modellunternehmen Global Bike und besteht aus einer Einführung mittels Folien, einer Reihe von einfachen Übungen und einer Fallstudie. In den Übungen werden die Lernenden in die grundlegenden Funktionen der Prozessautomatisierung mit SAP Build Process Automation eingeführt. Sie lernen zu Beginn Anwendungen und Automationen am Beispiel eines einfachen Hello-World-Beispiels kennen. 

In der Fallstudie durchlaufen die Lernenden ein komplexes Projekt, welches ihnen zeigt, wie Global Bike die geplante Verfügbarkeit von Leihfahrrädern an den Standorten New York City und München basierend auf der Wettervorhersage für die kommenden Tage plant. Dazu liest die zu entwickelnde Automation Temperaturen, Tagesdaten und Orte von der Google-Wetterseite und verarbeitet diese weiter. Studierende lernen, wie einmal erstellte Automationen wiederholt und fehlerfrei die Daten beschaffen, aufbereiten und zu Unternehmensentscheidungen heranziehen.

Wir laden Sie herzlich ein, dieses spannende Curriculum näher kennenzulernen und gemeinsam die Möglichkeiten von SAP BTP Process Automation zu entdecken.

Melden Sie sich jetzt für unsere kostenfreie SAP BTP Schulung an:

  • 8. November: SAP BTP Process Automation

Bei Interesse wenden Sie sich bitte an customer-relations@ucc.ovgu.de.

Viele Grüße das Team vom SAP UCC Magdeburg

entry.title: How to control externally the iflow in integration suite?
entry.content:
How to control externally the iflow in integration suite? i.e. What is the substitute of external channel concept of SAP PO in SAP Integration Suite? 

We have current scenario in PO that we are externally controlling the channels from ABAP program. How we can achieve this in Integration Suite?

entry.title: Grant users read-only access to the context of a sap build process automation workflow
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently facing the issue, that I want to grant read-only access to the context of a sap process automation (SBPA) workflow to my end users, via a REST call. Hereby, I find myself in the following scenario. I created a custom UI5-Application that uses the instance-id of a workflow to display the context. Of course, I want to design the authorization in such a way that the user is not able to manipulate the call to the SBPA API by,  for example, sending a post request via Postman.

To implement this, I have evaluated the following options, which have not led to a satisfactory result:

  • Create a destination with client id and client secret of the SBPA service intance
    • By doing this, I enable the user to access the context but also to take the called URL and send a post request, even though the actual credentials are concealed in the destination. 
  • Use API Key's within SBPA
    • I was not able to get them to grant access at all. In addition, I am unsettled by the following statement in this documentation on API keys: "You only need to use API keys for triggers that run automations."

Maybe I am missing something or one of you has another idea and can guide me into the right direction.

Thanks in advance,
Lukas
